title: Trollius anemonifolius
intro: Trollius anemonifolius, also known as globe-flower or anemone-flowered globe-flower, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and relieving pain, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. The bioactive constituents of Trollius anemonifolius include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, such as isorhapontigenin, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Trollius anemonifolius is commonly used in traditional medicine to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are employed to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Trollius anemonifolius is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It is commonly known as the anemone-leaved globeflower or anemone-leaved trollius. As a member of the Ranunculaceae family, it is classified as a perennial plant that thrives in moist environments. Trollius anemonifolius is native to mountainous regions of the Himalayas and surrounding areas. Its classification places it among the numerous species of Ranunculaceae, which are often referred to as the buttercup family.
